task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened the correct scene 'dwanko_run.tnz' in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application window is open, and the title bar, Xsheet, or project browser clearly displays 'dwanko_run.tnz'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent added a Particles effect to the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX Schematic or Xsheet shows a 'Particles' node or column added to the scene. The viewer may also show a particle effect (like snow or sparkles).

task_completion.description: Verify the particles are correctly overlaid on the existing character animation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the FX Schematic, the Particles effect is connected to the Output node alongside or over the character animation. In the viewer, particles are visibly rendering on top of the character.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configured the output settings to render a PNG sequence to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' window is open, with the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/particle_snow/' (or ending in 'particle_snow') and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent rendered the composite result as a PNG sequence of at least 24 frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar shows completion of at least 24 frames, or a file manager/terminal window displays multiple generated PNG files (e.g., 0001.png to 0024.png) in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/particle_snow/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to add the effect and render, rather than running a script or terminal command to bypass the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenToonz interface, such as clicking menus, wiring nodes in the schematic, or adjusting output settings.

integrity.description: The agent actually rendered the scene from OpenToonz rather than copying pre-existing images.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz render progress window is visible during the trajectory, or the output settings exactly match the generated files.

integrity.description: The agent applied the effect within the software rather than editing the project file with a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The FX schematic or Xsheet in OpenToonz visually displays the added particle effect node/column being manipulated.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct scene is 'dwanko_run.tnz' located in '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/'. The output directory must be '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/particle_snow/'. The scene has 72 frames in total, and the agent must render at least 24 frames as a PNG sequence. The rendered output should visually contain snow or sparkle particles overlaid on the character.
